decoded.explanation: The defendant is a first-time offender who committed a non-violent financial crime and has demonstrated remorse and an attempt at restitution by paying back part of the funds. While embezzlement is a serious breach of trust, the guidelines typically favor shorter sentences for non-violent, first-time white-collar offenses. The spectator's outburst is disregarded as it is extraneous to the legal facts of the case. A sentence of 8 months is sufficient to address the punitive aspect of the law while allowing for rehabilitation.
